goal: Close the 2.4 s sim-faster-than-real cycle-time gap (11.8 s real vs 9.4 s sim) on an IRB 6700 cell by matching ABB rated drive limits, adding blending zones, modeling I/O wait times — not a 1.25x fudge factor.
thoughts: The gap is mechanistic: sim hits idealized drive maxVelocity on every segment while the real ABB controller enforces rated vel/accel/jerk + blending zones AND the real cell has fieldbus I/O latency (gripper actuate ~300 ms etc.) that is zero in sim. Four attribution buckets: drive vel, drive accel, blending/waypoint approach, I/O waits. Calibrate drive limits first via a single-point-to-point ISO 9283 move, THEN full cycle. A 1.25x fudge defeats the purpose of the sim.
code:
# 1. Pull ABB rated spec per joint (vel, accel, max effort)
lookup_product_spec(query="ABB IRB 6700 235/2.65 rated joint velocity acceleration data sheet manual")

# 2. Inspect current drives (sim default is often higher than ABB rated)
list_all_prims(root="/World/IRB_6700", schema_filter="PhysicsDriveAPI")

# 3. Match drive maxVelocity + maxForce to ABB spec per joint
# (pseudocode loop -- Kenji sets exact values from the manual)
for joint_name, (v_max, f_max) in {"<J1>":("<V1>","<F1>"), "<J2>":("<V2>","<F2>")}.items():
    set_attribute(prim_path=f"/World/IRB_6700/{joint_name}",
                  attribute="drive:angular:physics:maxVelocity", value=v_max)
    set_attribute(prim_path=f"/World/IRB_6700/{joint_name}",
                  attribute="drive:angular:physics:maxForce",    value=f_max)

# 4. Blending zones / waypoint approach -- Isaac Sim motion-blend patterns
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim motion blending waypoint approach zone Lula RMPflow ABB fine fly-by")

# 5. Inject I/O wait model into the motion script (real-world, zero in sim by default)
run_usd_script(script="""
IO_WAITS_MS = {
    'gripper_close': 300,   # real: pneumatic gripper actuate + fieldbus
    'gripper_open':  250,
    'vacuum_on':     200,
    'vacuum_off':    150,
}
# wrap each motion-script I/O call with a sim-side sleep for IO_WAITS_MS[event]/1000.0
# Also inject the 300 ms fieldbus latency on every I/O event if modeling comms.
print('I/O wait model injected -- rerun cycle, expect +~1s on gripper events alone')
""")

# 6. Calibration protocol (ISO 9283 style):
#    a. Single point-to-point move at rated speed -- calibrate drive limits first
#    b. Add blending zones and re-measure
#    c. Add I/O waits and re-measure
#    d. Report cycle-time attribution per bucket; target gap -> +/-5%
# Do NOT multiply sim time by 1.25 -- that is a fudge factor, not a model.
failure_modes: Applying a 1.25x fudge — defeats the purpose of the sim, cannot generalize
Tuning all four buckets simultaneously — can't attribute which fix contributed what
Modeling drives but ignoring I/O waits — still 1-2 s fast, leaves the gap unclosed
Using whatever default maxVelocity exists instead of ABB rated spec from the manual
